title: Defrauded I a Butterfly -- by Emily Dickinson — Deep Poetry Lines
content:
Defrauded I a Butterfly --
The lawful Heir -- for Thee --
meaning: "Defrauded I a Butterfly --" by Emily Dickinson is a deep poem consisting of 2 lines. The poet explores themes of deep through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "Defrauded I a Butterfly -- The lawful Heir -- for Thee --...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Emily Dickinson's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
